candidates.why: For a bank, this appears to be the closest top-line revenue equivalent, summing net interest income and non-interest income.

variables.notes: Candidate list was empty. For banking statements there is often no standard 'Revenue' row; 'Total operating income' is the best available top-line proxy from the Income Statement preview.
No clear COGS/cost of sales concept is present in this banking Income Statement. Banking statements typically do not report COGS.

candidates.why: Explicit SG&A row exists and is populated; per rules, prefer the explicit SG&A row only.
Overhead component, but should not be selected when an explicit populated SG&A row exists.

variables.notes: Explicit populated SG&A row exists, so selected only that row per hierarchy rule.

candidates.why: Income statement item, not balance sheet equity.

Equity subcomponent only, not total book equity.
Best match for book equity and appears to exclude non-controlling interests based on comparison with separate NCI and 'Total equity'.
Also total equity, but appears to include non-controlling interests in at least one period, so less preferred for BE.

variables.notes: Selected 'Total Equity' because it is the best book equity line and appears to exclude non-controlling interests, avoiding double counting with MIB.

Income statement allocation line, not preferred over balance sheet NCI for MIB.

variables.notes: Priority list uses the balance sheet NCI labels only. BE uses 'Total Equity', which appears to exclude NCI, so separate MIB is appropriate.
